message:
beads: close verification and replay tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","title":"Emit machine-readable diagnostic payloads with artifact pointers","description":"Produce structured payloads that preserve full scan detail without forcing operators to read wall-of-text output.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:08:59.580580025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:07:43.249496875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:07:43.248798056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:18.677649403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:08:59.580580025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:00.309905352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1308,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"These payloads should let other surfaces consume scan results directly, including:
- doctor
- startup summaries
- robot output
- health/system dashboards

They must also point at detail artifacts instead of duplicating everything inline.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","title":"Integrate scan summaries into doctor, startup, robot, and health surfaces","description":"Thread the concise diagnostic model into the operator surfaces that should surface archive hygiene information.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:00.554261769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.939695421Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.939498693Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:00.554261769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:01.299078389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1309,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead is about consistent operator experience.

The same scan findings should not look materially different depending on whether they are surfaced via doctor, startup, robot, or a health-oriented surface.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.4","title":"Document operator copy rules for concise terminal output versus full artifacts","description":"Define text rules that keep terminal output terse while ensuring detailed diagnostics remain available elsewhere.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:01.542468565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.932045033Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.931826193Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:01.542468565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:02.272787744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:02.577406391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1310,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should define when the system says:
- only the root cause
- root cause plus one-line summary of secondary warnings
- artifact path for full detail

The goal is to keep real incidents understandable under pressure.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","title":"Add golden tests for scan summary copy, severity bucketing, and safest next-action recommendations","description":"Protect the archive-scan diagnostic contract with golden fixtures and surface-level tests that verify concise copy, deduped severity assignment, artifact pointers, safest next actions, and structured logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.173417852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.985479941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:24.985279295Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.173417852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.664194673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.916803851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1344,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead keeps diagnostics genuinely helpful. It should prove that the system recommends the safest next action first, avoids contradictory copy across surfaces, and does not regress back into a wall of warnings plus vague artifact references.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:11Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","title":"Add golden tests for scan summary copy, severity bucketing, and safest next-action recommendations","description":"Protect the archive-scan diagnostic contract with golden fixtures and surface-level tests that verify concise copy, deduped severity assignment, artifact pointers, safest next actions, and structured logs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.173417852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:40.779475274Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.173417852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.664194673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:10.916803851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1344,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead keeps diagnostics genuinely helpful. It should prove that the system recommends the safest next action first, avoids contradictory copy across surfaces, and does not regress back into a wall of warnings plus vague artifact references.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","title":"Durability program: verification, regression tests, and residual-risk signoff","description":"Close the durability program with real-path verification, chaos coverage, rollout gates, telemetry, and an explicit residual-risk register.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:04.883016701Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:54.507717521Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","test","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:04.883016701Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:14.236253224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:14.556480543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:14.873892969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:15.187457887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:15.512994614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1301,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Intent

Close the durability program with proof, not vibes.

This lane exists to ensure every new durability path has:
- focused unit/integration coverage
- fault-injection and chaos coverage
- cross-surface operator verification
- explicit rollout gates and residual-risk accounting

## Verification Standard

Rust builds/checks/tests in this repo must run through `rch`, not locally. The resulting evidence should be strong enough that later contributors do not need to rediscover these failure classes by breaking a real mailbox.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:08:53Z"},{"id":1327,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Verification Hardening Note

The verification lane should now be read as carrying three simultaneous obligations:
- **correctness proof** for state-machine, recovery, and degraded-write behavior
- **operator-surface proof** that CLI/server/web/robot/TUI agree on mailbox state and next action
- **forensic logging proof** that every failing lane leaves behind enough evidence to diagnose the problem quickly

This is intentionally stronger than ordinary pass/fail testing.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","title":"State-machine unit and integration coverage for mailbox verdicts and transitions","description":"Build focused table-driven, property/fuzz, and journaling tests for mailbox states, verdict computation, transition rules, and degraded-write safety.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:07.488775055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.250322235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.250099087Z","close_reason":"Implemented in durability program work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:16.471247382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:07.488775055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:51.760537272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1316,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should cover healthy, stale, suspect, broken, degraded-read, and recovery transitions. The point is to prove the policy engine itself before relying on end-to-end behavior.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","title":"Author table-driven verdict-matrix tests for every archive, SQLite, and owner-state combination","description":"Enumerate the concrete combinations of archive inventory, SQLite schema or integrity state, lock owner, deleted-binary status, and recovery history that must map to each mailbox verdict.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:26.057664636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:26.378450364Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:50:26.378233208Z","close_reason":"Implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:26.703645628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:26.899137248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:27.104567528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:26.057664636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:26.506181523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1330,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Future implementers should treat this as the canonical state-classification matrix. If a new mailbox incident class appears in production, it should first be expressible as another row in this table before changing supervisor logic.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:27Z"}]}
@@ -1879,7 +1879,7 @@
 {"id":"br-rqv3i","title":"Durability program: improve startup and doctor messaging for root-cause clarity","description":"Make startup and doctor outputs lead with root cause, recovery mode, and artifact path instead of warning floods.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:19:56.538388695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:54.689801591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:13.576431250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:13.906140895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1300,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Intent

Clarify startup and doctor outputs so operators immediately understand the blocking root cause instead of getting buried in secondary warnings.

## Output Principles

- lead with the stop condition
- keep warning floods terse in terminal output while preserving full detail in artifacts
- surface archive hygiene issues separately from live corruption/recovery incidents
- point directly at the relevant forensic bundle or detail artifact

## Why This Matters

The durability work only helps users if the operator-facing text reflects the actual control flow and diagnosis model. Otherwise we keep solving the wrong problem first.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:08:53Z"},{"id":1328,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Messaging Hardening Note

This lane is not merely cosmetic copy cleanup.

Its job is to encode three user-experience rules into the plan:
1. recoverable self-healing should stay quiet for normal users
2. operators should still get terse, high-value situational awareness
3. irrecoverable or safety-critical states should escalate clearly and with the right artifact pointers
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-rqv3i.1","title":"Order startup and doctor output around the primary root cause","description":"Make the first line of operator output identify the actual stop condition before any secondary warning set appears.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:02.824142446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:45:47.243499069Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:45:47.243290880Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit 2cf10435","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.1","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:02.824142446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1311,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should codify the principle that the first screenful of output answers: "why is the system blocked right now?"

Everything else is secondary.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:03Z"},{"id":1384,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Startup/Doctor Output Ordering Specification

### First-Line Principle

The first line of any startup or doctor output MUST answer:
> "What is preventing normal operation right now?"

If nothing is preventing normal operation, the first line says so.

### Output Order Template

\`\`\`
1. [STATUS] Primary verdict (Healthy/Stale/Suspect/Broken/Recovering/Escalate)
2. [ROOT CAUSE] If not healthy: the single blocking reason
3. [NEXT ACTION] If not healthy: what the operator should do
4. [ARTIFACT PATH] If incident: where detailed diagnostics are saved
5. --- separator ---
6. [SECONDARY] Warning list (collapsed by default in TUI, expanded in verbose)
7. [METRICS] Health metrics if healthy
\`\`\`

### Example Outputs

#### Healthy
\`\`\`
✓ Mailbox healthy (archive=1951, db=1951, sync=ok)
\`\`\`

#### Broken (Empty DB with Archive Data)
\`\`\`
✗ Mailbox broken: DB is empty but archive contains 1951 messages
  Root cause: schema_populated probe failed (sqlite_master has 0 tables)
  Next action: Run 'am doctor reconstruct' to rebuild from archive
  Incident bundle: /path/to/storage/incidents/2026-04-02T23:15:00Z.bundle/
---
2 additional warnings (run with --verbose to see)
\`\`\`

#### Recovering
\`\`\`
⟳ Mailbox recovering (owned by PID 12345 since 2026-04-02T23:10:00Z)
  Root cause: Previous session initiated recovery
  Next action: Wait for recovery to complete or check if PID 12345 is stale
  Lock file: /path/to/storage.sqlite3.recovery.lock
\`\`\`

#### Stale
\`\`\`
⚠ Mailbox stale: DB missing 150 messages present in archive
  Root cause: archive_db_parity probe failed (archive=1951, db=1801, diff=150)
  Next action: Normal operation allowed; background sync will catch up
---
WAL sidecar without SHM (expected for frankensqlite)
\`\`\`

### Implementation Rules

1. **Verdict first**: Always lead with the MailboxState from compute_mailbox_verdict()
2. **One root cause**: Extract the highest-severity failing probe's detail
3. **Actionable next step**: Map each state to a concrete operator action
4. **Artifact pointer**: If incident bundle exists, show path immediately
5. **Collapse secondary**: Warnings without immediate action go below separator
6. **Color coding**: ✓ green, ⚠ yellow, ✗ red, ⟳ cyan for terminal output
","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:30:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-rqv3i.2","title":"Separate archive hygiene debt from live mailbox incidents","description":"Ensure operators can distinguish non-blocking archive anomalies from live corruption, drift, lock, or recovery incidents.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:03.406148123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:22:51.191892527Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:22:51.191692703Z","close_reason":"Implemented in durability program commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.2","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:03.406148123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.2","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:04.004598838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1312,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Do not let archive hygiene warnings masquerade as the primary live failure.

This bead exists so people stop solving the wrong problem first.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:03Z"}]}

- current mode
- current owner
- next automatic step
- relevant bundle/detail artifact path
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:09:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-rqv3i.6","title":"Define silent-self-heal versus explicit-escalation policy for users and operators","description":"Specify when recovery stays invisible to end users, when operators get terse status, and when the system is allowed to escalate visibly.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.905206436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:59:31.974649596Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:59:31.973729042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:51:27.657805285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.905206436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:50.445834362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","depends_on_id":"br-rqv3i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:50.694823488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1325,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead aligns the messaging lane with the product promise that users should not be bothered by recoverable incidents.

It should define:
- which states are intentionally silent to normal users
- which states produce operator-only visibility
- which thresholds justify visible escalation
- what language distinguishes recoverable degradation from irrecoverable risk

The goal is to make "do not worry users unless necessary" an explicit rule instead of an intuition.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:50Z"},{"id":1367,"issue_id":"br-rqv3i.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"The explicit-escalation contract should name the only durability cases that justify bothering a human: archive truth is damaged or inconsistent, repeated candidate rebuilds fail without forward progress, or exclusive recovery ownership cannot be acquired beyond the configured long threshold. Ordinary stale, suspect, or broken SQLite states that are still self-healing should stay quiet except for terse operator-visible status.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:12:08Z"}]}
